rules.source_of_truth: This file is the source of truth for planned harness and product work.

features.title: Initialize minimal harness structure
features.user_visible_behavior: A fresh agent session can identify the repository purpose, startup command, verification command, current progress, and next action from repo files.

steps: Confirm required harness files exist.
Confirm init.sh is executable.
Confirm feature_list.json contains required fields.
Confirm AGENTS.md contains startup workflow, required artifacts, definition of done, and end-of-session instructions.
Confirm AGENTS.md links to startup and harness docs.

features.evidence: ./init.sh passed on 2026-05-25; scripts/verify-harness.sh reported: Harness verification passed. AGENTS.md hardened with end-of-session instructions on 2026-05-25.
features.notes: Created before the product stack is selected; later tightened against the provided AGENTS.md template.

features.title: Choose project direction and initial local learning stack
features.user_visible_behavior: A fresh agent session can identify that this project is a Mattermost ChatOps bot for authenticating users to a Kubernetes cluster-management backend, and can see the first local learning path.

steps: Record the chosen project goal in README.md and AGENTS.md.
Record the K3s-first and Mattermost-local learning path.
Record open decisions for bot runtime and backend API contract.
Keep host-level setup commands explicit instead of auto-installing from init.sh.
Run the updated init.sh successfully.

features.evidence: Project direction recorded on 2026-05-26 in README.md, AGENTS.md, docs/project-direction.md, docs/local-environment.md, DECISIONS.md, and PROGRESS.md. ./init.sh passed on 2026-05-26.
features.notes: The backend API and bot implementation runtime remain intentionally undecided; they are tracked as later work.

features.title: Verify local K3s access
features.user_visible_behavior: A developer can confirm local Kubernetes access with K3s and see recorded evidence of node, namespace, and cluster-info checks.

steps: Confirm the local K3s installation or access method.
Run kubectl or sudo k3s kubectl node and namespace checks.
Record exact commands and summarized output in PROGRESS.md.
Update docs/local-environment.md with any machine-specific setup notes.
Run ./init.sh successfully.

features.evidence: On 2026-05-26, installed k3d 5.8.3 with Homebrew and created local cluster csit-lab with one server and one agent. ./scripts/verify-k3s.sh passed for kubectl context k3d-csit-lab. Evidence: kubectl cluster-info reached the control plane; kubectl get nodes -o wide showed k3d-csit-lab-server-0 and k3d-csit-lab-agent-0 Ready on K3s v1.33.6+k3s1; kubectl get namespaces showed default, kube-node-lease, kube-public, and kube-system Active.
features.notes: Local macOS path uses Docker Desktop plus k3d instead of a host-level K3s service. Do not auto-install or start K3s from init.sh.

features.title: Deploy first K3s learning workload
features.user_visible_behavior: A developer can deploy, inspect, log, and clean up a simple workload in a local K3s namespace.

steps: Create a learning namespace.
Deploy a simple workload.
Expose or inspect the workload.
Collect kubectl get, describe, and logs evidence.
Clean up the learning namespace.
Record evidence in PROGRESS.md.
Run ./init.sh successfully.

features.evidence: On 2026-05-26, used kubectl context k3d-csit-lab to create namespace csit-lab, deploy hello-nginx from nginx:stable, expose it on port 80, and verify rollout. Evidence: rollout status reported deployment "hello-nginx" successfully rolled out; kubectl get all --namespace csit-lab -o wide showed pod 1/1 Running, service hello-nginx ClusterIP on 80/TCP, deployment 1/1 available, and matching ReplicaSet 1/1 ready; kubectl logs --namespace csit-lab deploy/hello-nginx showed nginx startup completed. Cleanup evidence: kubectl delete namespace csit-lab completed, and kubectl get namespace csit-lab returned NotFound.
features.notes: Depends on L01. The learning namespace was intentionally removed after verification.

features.title: Set up local Mattermost
features.user_visible_behavior: A developer can start local Mattermost, create a team and channel, and identify the local URL for integration testing.

steps: Choose the local Mattermost deployment path.
Start Mattermost locally.
Create or confirm a local team and channel.
Record URL, deployment command, and cleanup command.
Run ./init.sh successfully.

features.evidence: On 2026-05-26, selected the official Mattermost Docker Compose deployment without NGINX for first local familiarisation after checking current Mattermost docs and after user clarified that the setup should resemble production more closely than the preview image. Cloned https://github.com/mattermost/docker to /private/tmp/mattermost-docker-csit, configured .env with DOMAIN=localhost and MM_SERVICESETTINGS_SITEURL=http://localhost:8065, created the Mattermost volume directories, and added a local Apple Silicon override with platform: linux/amd64 because mattermost/mattermost-enterprise-edition:11.7.0 did not publish a linux/arm64/v8 manifest. Docker Compose started Postgres and Mattermost; docker compose ps showed mattermost-docker-csit-mattermost-1 Up and healthy on 0.0.0.0:8065->8065/tcp and postgres Up. curl http://localhost:8065/api/v4/system/ping returned HTTP/1.1 200 OK with JSON status OK and Mattermost version 11.7.0. ./scripts/verify-mattermost.sh passed with host-local access. Created local admin username csit-admin, team csit-lab with id bfnj9akc4fytxp4gwbn9ak5cer, and channel chatops-lab with id ez1crw7et3fztdsxywargec1sr.
features.notes: Depends on L01 or L02 only if Mattermost will be deployed into K3s; otherwise it can be done with Docker independently. Current chosen path is the official mattermost/docker checkout with docker compose -f docker-compose.yml -f docker-compose.without-nginx.yml up -d. On Apple Silicon, include a local override file that sets services.mattermost.platform to linux/amd64. The current checkout and local data live under /private/tmp/mattermost-docker-csit.

features.title: Explore Mattermost bot integration options
features.user_visible_behavior: The repository documents the recommended first integration style for the ChatOps bot and why it fits the authentication workflow.

steps: Review slash commands, webhooks, bot accounts, plugins, and apps.
Record tradeoffs for authentication and local development.
Choose the first prototype integration style.
Record the decision in DECISIONS.md.
Run ./init.sh successfully.

features.notes: Backend API contract can remain mocked or pending during this feature.

features.title: Choose and scaffold bot implementation runtime
features.user_visible_behavior: The repository contains the first bot skeleton with documented install, test, lint, build, and local run commands.

steps: Choose the bot runtime and framework.
Record versions and rationale.
Scaffold minimal bot source code.
Add install, test, lint, build, and local run commands.
Run ./init.sh successfully.

features.notes: Depends on L03 and L04. The backend API may be mocked if the real contract is still unavailable.
